items.title: Earlier caffeine cutoff
items.hypothesis: Stopping caffeine at noon instead of 14:00 improves sleep quality scores by at least 1 point.
items.null_hypothesis: Moving the caffeine cutoff from 14:00 to 12:00 does not change sleep quality.

items.title: Post-workout protein timing
items.hypothesis: Consuming 30g protein within 30 minutes of strength training reduces next-day soreness.
items.null_hypothesis: Protein timing within 30 minutes post-workout does not affect next-day soreness.
items.intervention: 30g whey protein shake immediately after every strength session.
items.analysis_summary: Average soreness dropped from 6.2 to 4.1. Recovery readiness improved from 5.5 to 7.0. Compliance was 85%. Results suggest a meaningful effect but the sample size is small.
items.next_action: Continue the protocol and re-evaluate after 30 more days of data.
